highlights.text: I wrote at 4pm on Monday’s close” “My general lean for tomorrow is ES can do some more back-testing.

6068-71 is 1st up, and if bulls are really energized, can make the run for 6082 then 6115-20” and “For now though, the bull case is that we got a short squeeze trigger on the Failed Breakdown of 5997 this morning at 8am, which triggered longs.
The bull case ES can now begin back-testing with 6068-71 1st up.
Today’s FOMC plan was all about 1 level: 6066-70 and I wrote yesterday: “On a normal day though, the bull case is simple that ES defends 6066-70 ideally and build a base above it.

This week started off with my absolute favorite combination of price action: As I’ve discussed for days ES has two things it loves to do when volatility ramps: It goes “elevator down” which refers to few hour free falls like we saw right off the Sunday gap down open, and then it puts my core setup - the Failed Breakdown - and initiates a face ripping short squeeze.
After 170+ point “elevator down” sell to start the week, we got a massive Failed Breakdown, and as provided to readers, longs were triggered.
In this case, the Failed Breakdown was of last Mondays ~5997 low.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the combination of Failed Breakdowns (my core setup) that triggered the monster long into FOMC.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled For A Big Move. What Way? Jan 30th Plan
